canonical_definition: The property of a model that allows its parameters to be represented with lower numerical precision while maintaining performance, indicating lower effective complexity.
papers: Compute-Optimal LLMs Provably Generalize Better with Scale
OSTQuant: Refining Large Language Model Quantization with Orthogonal and Scaling Transformations for Better Distribution Fitting

relationships.evidence: Empirically it has been observed that practical quantization algorithms also reveal that larger models are more quantizable... though the effect is not very pronounced with the GPTQ algorithm we use here.
